title: Piyush Chaudhari
text:
WelcomeWorkEducationProjectsPublications


I am a Data Analyst working at UF Health Integrated Data Repository. My work primarily focuses on designing and developing data science pipelines to improve the coverage and quality of the data, primarily for the OneFlorida+ Clinical Research Consortium. Additionally, I also work to improve research infrastructure at UF by providing consultation for research teams, supporting feasibility and cohort discovery, and fulfilling research data requests.
Previously, I have worked as a Statistical Data Analyst at Department of Epidemiology working on research projects with NIH, NCATS, CDC and NIDA to design targeted interventions.


Data Management Analyst III | UF Health IDR (02/23-now)

Liaison between IDR and OneFlorida+ Clinical Research Network
Design and develop data science pipelines to improve the coverage and quality of the data
Working as an honest broker for research data focusing on electronic health records
Running ETL pipelines for OneFlorida+ data refresh, All of Us Research Program, UF Health Tumor Registry, COVID-19 PCORI data refresh, National COVID Cohort Collaborative (N3C), OneFlorida+ i2b2 refresh and other data requests
Develop software and packages to run and validate data in OMOP Common Data Model
Conduct OneFlorida+ i2b2 trainings and workshops for hospital and research partners and working with OneFlorida+ Coordinating Center to process new user provisioning

Data Management Analyst II | UF Department of Epidemiology (06/18 - 02/23)

Lead Statistician on projects with NIH, NCATS, CDC and NIDA to design targeted interventions
Created visualizations, hotspots and heatmap analysis for community intervention planning



Master of Science in Biostatistics
University of Florida | Gainesville, FL (2016-2018)
Bachelor of Technology in Biotechnology
Vellore Institute of Technology | India (2012 - 2016)


Coming up ……

J Community Health. 2023 Apr;48(2):338-346. doi: 10.1007/s10900-022-01169-2. Epub 2022 Nov 27. PMID: 36436165; PMCID: PMC9702843.
Cheng TD, Chaudhari PV, Bitsie KR, Striley CW, Varma DS, Cottler LB. The HealthStreet Cancer Survivor Cohort: a Community Registry for Cancer Research. J Cancer Surviv. 2022 Jan 28:10.1007/s11764-022-01173-4. doi: 10.1007/s11764-022-01173-4. Epub ahead of print. PMID: 35089522; PMCID: PMC9329490.

title: Piyush Chaudhari
text:
Quartoing



Quarto


R


Website



The journey of procrastination and a young inspiration



Piyush Chaudhari


Oct 16, 2023








No matching items

title: Quartoing
text:
I have always preached that in this century and this year and all years forward, every should have a personal website. A part of it is my own leaning towards reading something on my phone, tablet or computer and not requiring any sort of app or software installation (in a sense, PDFs have overcome this limitation). But a more significant part of it is establishing a presence in this big village of world wide web where people perceive you more than what you show on it. What better way to put your work in the center of it..
Obviously, preaching doesn’t get things done, and thankfully so. So while for a long time, I have been giving these sermons about the importance of having a website, I never took the plunge myself. When I look back, I cannot come up with a better explanation than a simple fact that I was letting perfect be the enemy of good. It came with a good lesson- if you think something is a good idea, go ahead and do it.
The inspiration came from a fresh angle- my friend Katie. She was one of the people I did give this sermon to, and she just made it a project for herself. I wasn’t much surprised given her work ethic is unparalleled, and she doesn’t stop until she figures out how to execute something that interests her. Very proudly, she went ahead and created her Quarto website way before me. You can check it out here. Its still a work in progress, like mine is, and like most of the world is.
